slides.title: Natural Resources and Sustainability
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 8 English, Unit 5: Natural Resources and Sustainability. Welcome to Unit 5 of your Year 8 English course. In this unit, you will explore the important topic of natural resources and sustainability. You will learn how communities around the world—and especially in East Africa—depend on natural resources like water, fish, forests, and clean air, and what happens when those resources are threatened by pollution, carelessness, or outdated practices. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Understand and use 31 new vocabulary words related to thought, strategy, environment, and persuasion in your reading, writing, and speaking
Identify and construct complex noun phrases using pre-modifiers (adjectives, determiners) and post-modifiers (prepositional phrases, relative...
Form and use the future passive voice (will be + past participle) to describe actions that will happen in the future
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to understand and use 31 new vocabulary words related to thought, strategy, environment, and persuasion in your reading, writing, and speaking. you will also identify and construct complex noun phrases using pre-modifiers (adjectives, determiners) and post-modifiers (prepositional phrases, relative clauses, participle phrases). you will also form and use the future passive voice (will be + past participle) to describe actions that will happen in the future. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: falter, pristine, deceptive, naive, antonym, tactic. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Natural Resources and Sustainability: source text 1
Natural Resources and Sustainability: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Natural Resources and Sustainability: source text 1 and Natural Resources and Sustainability: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: What Is a Complex Noun Phrase?
bullets: A complex noun phrase is a group of words built around a head noun that gives detailed information about a person, place, thing, or idea. Instead of using...
slides.narration: The first language focus is What Is a Complex Noun Phrase?. A complex noun phrase is a group of words built around a head noun that gives detailed information about a person, place, thing, or idea. Instead of using a single noun on its own, you add words before it (pre-modifiers) or after it (post-modifiers) to make the description richer and more precise. For example, compare these two sentences: Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Story Discussion. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
